w:r.w:t: Not recorded — visible in Google Cloud Console

The Travel & Finance PWA Suite consists of two independent Progressive Web Applications built for personal and family use. Both apps are fully serverless, using Google Drive as a JSON file backend and Google OAuth 2.0 for authentication. The entire project is hosted on GitHub Pages at zero cost.
Zero server cost — no VPS, no database, no backend hosting
Touch-first mobile UX — pill selectors for all repetitive inputs
Offline-capable — IndexedDB caching with Drive sync on reconnect
4–5 family members with shared travel data and per-person document tracking
Private financial data protected by 4-digit PIN in a separate PWA install

Primary user: Anees (owner, aneesaluva@gmail.com). Family members can be added as test users in Google Cloud Console and assigned Admin or Viewer roles within the app. Maximum 100 test users in Testing mode.
Vanilla JavaScript (ES Modules), HTML5, CSS3 — no build step required
Tailwind CSS via CDN — utility-first, touch-optimised
Service Worker + IndexedDB — app works without internet, syncs on reconnect
Google Drive API — two JSON files (travel_data.json, finance_data.json)
Google OAuth 2.0 — Implicit Flow (response_type=token). No client secret needed.
Google Calendar API — document expiry reminders
SheetJS (XLSX.js) — client-side Excel generation, no server
jsPDF — A5 contact cards for people profiles
manifest.json + Service Worker — installable on Android and PC

Blueprint v1.0 specified PKCE flow. This was changed because PKCE requires a client secret which cannot safely be stored in a public GitHub repo. The final implementation uses OAuth 2.0 Implicit Flow (response_type=token). The access token is returned directly in the URL hash after sign-in. No client secret is used. The Google OAuth client type must be "Web Application" in Cloud Console.
Google Drive API, Google Calendar API
Testing mode — users must be added to Test Users list
All data lives in Google Drive under a dedicated folder named TravelFinanceApp/. Two JSON files store all app data:
travel_data.json — App A data: members, trips, documents, family relations, family defaults
finance_data.json — App B data: transactions, categories, accounts
Mirror folder TravelFinanceApp_Mirror/ — tiered backup: last 5 saves (edits/), last 5 edit-days (daily/), last 3 months (monthly/)
File IDs are stored in localStorage after first run so no re-search is needed on subsequent visits. On a new device, the app finds files by searching the Drive folder by name.
The app uses a local-first save flow implemented in shared/sync-manager.js:
localSave(appName, mergeFn) writes to IndexedDB in under 10ms — UI is never blocked
A background Drive queue processes writes sequentially
Write-ahead safe snapshot stored in IndexedDB before every Drive write
Boot integrity check: detects interrupted writes, stuck queues, record count anomalies
Sync status indicator: Synced / Syncing / N pending / Failed
Each version stamps a unique cache name in both service workers:

The version is stamped by /tmp/stamp_version.py on every build. If the cache name does not change between deploys, the service worker serves stale files. This caused multiple recurring errors in this build session — see Section 8.
Shared modules used by both apps
Live status cards per member — current location (India/Qatar), days in location, next document expiry with countdown. Red badge if doc expires within 30 days.
Full scrollable trip history, newest first. Filter by person and year. Tap row → view mode. FAB to add new trip. Pagination: 25 per page.
Step form: Person → Dates → Flights → Reason → Travel With. View mode by default. Edit button unlocks. Share button sends WhatsApp-formatted text.
Cards grouped by person. Life-bar (green/amber/red). 90/60/30-day alert toggles. Google Calendar sync toggle. Tap → view mode.
Person pill, doc type (with custom types inline), number, expiry date, alert toggles, calendar sync. Custom doc types saveable.
Family member cards with location badge, document expiry warning, contact count. Tap ⚙️ header icon for Person Management.
Full profile: photo, blood group, occupation, location tabs (Qatar/India addresses with photos), emergency contacts, QR code, PDF export.
Shared Qatar/India addresses with photos, shared emergency contacts, Plus Code support, contact picker.
Four tabs: People (members + manage access), Data (backup/restore/import), Security (sessions + activity log), Account (sign out + app info).
PDF, Excel, CSV, or WhatsApp summary of trip history.
Column mapping UI, preview 5 rows, auto-creates members for unknown names, duplicate detection by personId+dateOutIndia.
Rename person (updates all trips/docs), Merge A→B (all data reassigned, A deleted), Levenshtein duplicate detection.
Live status + filter bar (Person, Year, Doc status, Location)
Roles are stored in travel_data.json under data.roles: { email: "admin"|"viewer" }. The signed-in account email is checked on boot via resolveRole() in roles.js.
If data.roles is empty → first run → all users are Admin
If user email is not in roles list → treated as Admin (owner assumption)
If user email is explicitly set to "viewer" → Viewer restrictions apply
Viewer: cannot add, edit, delete, import, export, or backup
Admin role shown in Settings → Account → App Info for verification
IMPORTANT: To give family members Viewer access, they must ALSO be added as Test Users in Google Cloud Console (Testing mode limit: 100 users).
Import Path Depths (MUST NOT BE CHANGED)

app-a-family-hub/js/screens/*.js → must use ../../../shared/ app-a-family-hub/js/*.js → must use ../../shared/ app-a-family-hub/index.html → must use ../shared/
Same pattern applies to app-b-private-vault/. Wrong depths cause silent module load failures.
4-digit PIN with SHA-256 hash + random per-device salt stored in localStorage. 5 wrong attempts = 30-second lockout. Forgot PIN = re-authenticate Google.
Summary cards: Income, Spend, Net Balance by currency (QAR/INR/USD tabs). Filter bar (year, month, category, account). Recent transactions. Share via image or text.
Full scrollable list with filter bar. Year/month/category/account filters. Infinite scroll (30/page). Swipe-to-delete. Running balance. Export button.
Date, description (smart-search), amount spend, income, currency pill, category 1+2 pills, notes, account pill. Up to 3 photo slots per transaction.
View mode with WhatsApp copy and photo thumbnails.
Monthly bar chart (Income vs Spend), category donut, year-over-year comparison. Year filter excludes 1899 (Excel serial date bug).
Filter by Year/Month/Currency/Category1/Category2 → Download .xlsx or Email with attachment. Column order fixed.

Settings → Data → Manage Categories. Full screen. Search. Actions per category: Rename (updates all transactions), Merge into (reassigns + deletes source), Reassign all, Delete (only if 0 transactions).
Four tabs: Data (backup/import/restore/categories), Export (xlsx with filters), Security (sessions + activity log), Account (change PIN + app info).
Summary cards + filter bar + recent transactions
PIN hashed with SHA-256 + random per-device salt
Salt and hash stored in localStorage
Forgot PIN: re-authenticates with Google, allows PIN reset — no data loss
App-level config (including PIN-related data) stored in Drive via app-config.js
Categories are stored as a flat array in finance_data.json under data.categories. Default categories are hardcoded in settings.js and category-manager.js:
Food, Groceries, Rent, Salary, Transport, Medical, Education, Shopping, Utilities, Travel, Entertainment, Transfer, Investment, Insurance, Freelance, Other
Category 1 and Category 2 are independent free fields on each transaction — there is no parent-child relationship. Both are managed through the Category Manager screen.
name, emoji, color, photo, headOfHousehold, bloodGroup, occupation
personId, dateOutIndia, dateInQatar, dateOutQatar, dateInIndia, flights, reason, travelWith
personId, docName, docNumber, expiryDate, alertDays, calSynced, calEventId, photos
Timestamp of last successful Drive write
date, description, amountSpend, income, currency, category1, category2, notes1, account, photos (max 3)
Custom + default categories merged at render time
Custom accounts. Defaults: Cash, Card, Bank, Other

When importing from Excel, dates can arrive as serial number 0 (representing 1899-12-30). Both transactions.js and analytics.js filter out years outside 2000–2100. Boot integrity in index.html also repairs existing bad-date transactions on load. If this bug reappears, check the import-tool.js buildRecord() function which converts Excel serial dates.
User clicks "Sign in with Google" button
Google redirects back to the app URL with access_token in the URL hash (#access_token=...)
auth.js parses the hash, stores token and user info in localStorage
Token used in Authorization: Bearer headers for all API calls
On return visit: token checked from localStorage, refreshed if expired
First launch only — folder named TravelFinanceApp
On app load + before every save
Used for document expiry alerts. Each document with alert toggles (90/60/30 days) creates separate calendar events titled "[Name] [DocType] expires in X days".
Calendar sync toggled ON for a document
Expiry date or alert days changed
Sync toggled OFF or document deleted
Google OAuth, Drive file creation, IndexedDB, PWA shell, Service Worker, routing
App A: Dashboard, Travel Log, Add/Edit Trip, Documents, Add/Edit Doc, Settings
App B: PIN lock, Dashboard, Add Transaction, Transaction List, Analytics, Export, Settings
Document expiry alerts, life-bar, Google Calendar API integration
CSV/Excel import tool with column mapping, preview, validation
Offline PWA, background sync queue, PWA install prompts, app icons
Two-tier backup, dashboard share, Excel email export, rich filter bars, People management, Category manager
OAuth implicit flow fix — replaced PKCE with response_type=token
Local-first architecture, sync-manager.js, write-ahead snapshots, boot integrity
View mode for person/doc/trip, PC photo picker, WhatsApp share sheets
1899 year bug from Excel serial dates — guard + UI filter + boot repair
Full audit — renamed sheets, fix arguments.callee, merge duplicate imports
Import toast+auto-nav, transactions smart year fallback, vault save-image sheet
All year filter bug (month=0 explicit), transactions year in activeCount, clear-all button
setCurrentRole wired in showApp(), view mode fixes for doc+trip
All time filter clears year+month, import fullscreen overlay
activeYear null default, import member name hint on preview
Import auto-creates members — removed name matching requirement entirely
Person Management screen — rename, merge, Levenshtein duplicate detection
Category Manager dedicated screen — rename, merge, reassign, delete, search
Service worker cache bust (v2→v3), all files stamped for GitHub detection
Correct version stamp — re-stamp all 74 files
SW cache versioned per build, clear-all event delegation, export cat1+cat2 filters
renderSettings missing closing brace, transactions fallback infinite loop
Import status visible (modal not overlay), admin role default, paste direct listener
renderSettings closing brace (again), clear-all preserves currency
Full rewrite of App A settings.js, vault security import, analytics 1899
localSave import path fixed, openSecurityDashboard import, transactions retry on null data

These three issues were reported repeatedly throughout the build session (15+ times each). Multiple fix attempts were made but the issues persisted to end of session. The root causes are understood but the fixes did not hold due to structural issues in the codebase.
8.1 Vault App — Transactions Tab Blank / Inactive
Reported: 15+ times. Status: Unresolved in v3.5.5
Records tab shows filter bar (QAR/INR/USD tabs, "All transactions") but the transaction list below is empty. Clicking/tapping transactions does nothing. Filters do not respond.
Primary cause: getCachedFinanceData() returns null when the Drive sync has not completed yet
showApp() in index.html fires at line 208 when cached data exists — but on first open or after cache clear, it also fires at line 228 after Drive fetch completes
If the Records tab is opened before the Drive fetch completes (line 215–228 async block), getCachedFinanceData() returns null
The retry logic added in v3.5.5 (3 retries × 600ms) should help but depends on Drive response time
Secondary cause: Year filter defaulting to currentYear() caused the smart fallback to loop infinitely — fixed in v3.5.0
Service worker caching caused old transactions.js to be served — fixed in v3.4.9
v3.3.9: Smart year fallback added — caused infinite loop instead
v3.4.0: activeYear default changed to null — reduced loop but blank remained
v3.4.9: Event delegation on clear button — did not fix blank list
v3.5.0: Removed infinite loop in smart fallback (!!activeYear condition)
v3.5.5: Added 3-retry logic when getCachedFinanceData returns null — may not be enough
In app-b-private-vault/index.html, ensure showApp() is only called ONCE, after Drive data is confirmed loaded
Add a loading spinner between auth completion and screen render — do not render transactions until data is confirmed non-null
Consider adding a data-ready event that transactions.js listens for, rather than polling with retries
window.dispatchEvent(new Event("finance-data-ready")) after setCachedFinanceData(data) in index.html
In renderTransactions, await new Promise(r => window.addEventListener("finance-data-ready", r, {once:true})) if data is null
8.2 Travel App — Excel Import Not Working
Reported: 15+ times. Status: Likely working in v3.5.5 but unconfirmed by user testing
User uploads Excel file, maps columns, clicks "Import X Records", but no trips appear in Travel Log. No visible error message.
v3.5.3 and earlier: import overlay was a fullscreen dark div that appeared and dismissed in 1.5 seconds — user could not see it
v3.5.4: settings.js was rewritten, localSave imported from wrong file (drive.js instead of sync-manager.js) — caused import to throw silently
v3.5.5: localSave import fixed to sync-manager.js — import should now work
Name matching was removed in v3.4.4 — members are auto-created if not found
Status is shown in the modal's import-status div — check if it shows success/failure text
v3.3.6 to v3.3.9: Fuzzy name matching, auto-dispatch import:complete, scroll to done screen
v3.4.2: Replaced done screen with fullscreen overlay — invisible in <2 seconds
v3.4.4: Removed name matching entirely — auto-create members
v3.5.1: Replaced overlay with inline status bar in modal
v3.5.4: Full settings.js rewrite — introduced wrong import path
v3.5.5: Fixed localSave import from correct file (sync-manager.js)
Open Settings → Data → Import from Excel/CSV
Upload an Excel with columns: Name of Person, Date Out India, Date In Qatar
Map columns, click Preview, then click Import
Watch the import-status div at top of modal — should show "Saving X trips…" then "✅ X trips imported!"
If nothing shows, open browser console and look for errors — most likely a module import error
8.3 Vault App — Security & Manage Access Inactive
Reported: Multiple times. Status: Import fixed in v3.5.5 — needs user verification
Settings → Security tab → "Security & Access" row is visible but tapping it does nothing. App A: Settings → People tab → "Manage Access" row is visible but tapping it does nothing.
App B: openSecurityDashboard() was called but never imported in settings.js — fixed in v3.5.5
App A: Manage Access button is inside ${isAdmin() ? ... : ""} template — only shows if isAdmin() returns true at render time
isAdmin() depends on setCurrentRole() being called in showApp() before Settings renders
roles.js resolveRole() changed to default unknown users to Admin in v3.5.1
v3.4.1: setCurrentRole wired in showApp(data) in index.html
v3.5.1: resolveRole() changed — unknown email = admin by default
v3.5.4: Settings.js rewritten but import for openSecurityDashboard still missing in App B
v3.5.5: Import added for openSecurityDashboard in App B settings.js
Open App B → Settings → Security tab → tap "Security & Access"
If still inactive, check browser console for "openSecurityDashboard is not defined"
Open App A → Settings → Account tab → App Info — check Role shows "👑 Admin"
If Role shows "👁 Viewer", open travel_data.json in Drive and check the roles field
node --check passes but the browser still throws — Template literals with nested braces pass syntax check but can confuse brace-counting tools. The real test is the browser console, not node --check.

Service worker serves stale files — If you change JS files but the SW cache name does not change, users get old code. Always change the CACHE_NAME in sw.js when deploying. The stamp script (/tmp/stamp_version.py) does this automatically.
localSave lives in sync-manager.js, NOT drive.js — drive.js has writeData(). These are different. localSave() writes to IndexedDB first, then queues Drive write. writeData() goes directly to Drive.
Import path depth matters — screens/ files need ../../../shared/, not ../../shared/. Wrong paths cause silent module failures with no error in some browsers.

Two listeners on same button = two modals — Multiple addEventListeners on the same element accumulate. Always check if an element was already bound before adding listeners. Use a data attribute flag or remove-then-add pattern.
getCachedFinanceData() can return null on first open — Drive fetch is async. The transactions screen must not assume data exists immediately.
OAuth is Implicit Flow, not PKCE — Do not change this. PKCE requires a client secret. The client secret cannot be stored safely in a public repo.
isAdmin() reads a module-level variable set by setCurrentRole() — If Settings renders before setCurrentRole() is called, isAdmin() returns false and the Manage Access button is hidden.
Fix transactions blank screen properly — implement a finance-data-ready event in index.html as described in Section 8.1. This is the most impactful fix.
Verify import end-to-end — open browser console during an import and watch for errors. The localSave import path is now correct but verify the full chain.
Test Manage Access and Security in App B after v3.5.5 deploy — these should now work with the correct imports.
Add a simple test harness — even a manual test checklist in a markdown file would have caught many of these regressions. The recurring issues would have been caught immediately.
Create a data layer module — getCachedTravelData/getCachedFinanceData + localSave should be wrapped in a single data.js module that handles the "data not ready" case uniformly.
Centralise event binding — the pattern of getElementById + addEventListener repeated across multiple places is fragile. Consider a simple event bus or component registration pattern.

Add runtime import verification — a boot-time check that verifies all expected exports are available from each shared module would have caught the localSave and openSecurityDashboard errors immediately.
Consider a simple build step — even a 10-line script that bundles shared modules and validates imports would prevent the recurring "missing export" class of bugs without adding complexity.
The localSave → Drive queue pattern is solid — do not replace it with direct Drive writes for user-initiated actions
The mirror backup system (edits/daily/monthly tiers) works well — do not break it by changing the Drive file structure
IndexedDB keys: STORES.travel = "travel", STORES.finance = "finance", within key "data" — changing these breaks all existing user caches
The OAuth token refresh is in shared/auth.js — do not add token logic elsewhere
Both apps share auth-config.js for the Client ID — change it in both places if the Google project changes
Run python3 /tmp/stamp_version.py X.Y.Z YYYY-MM-DD before every package
This stamps version headers on all 51 JS/HTML/CSS files, updates both manifests, and versions both SW cache names
Push to GitHub Desktop — all 51+ files should show as changed
GitHub Pages deploys automatically from the main branch within 2–5 minutes
On first open after deploy, the service worker detects the new cache name and fetches fresh files
VERIFY: open app, check Settings → Account → App Info shows the new version number
VERIFY: open browser DevTools → Application → Service Workers — confirm new cache name is active
GitHub Desktop (manual push after each version)
2–5 minutes after push for GitHub Pages to update
Not recorded — check Google Cloud Console. Navigate to: console.cloud.google.com → project selector at top
NOT USED — Implicit Flow. Do not add client secret to any file.
Google Drive API v3, Google Calendar API v3

Go to Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → OAuth consent screen → Test users → Add users. Enter the Gmail address of the family member. They must be added here BEFORE they can sign in to the app. Maximum 100 test users in Testing mode. If you want unrestricted sign-in, go through Google's OAuth verification process (not recommended for a private family tool).
All app data is stored in the owner's Google Drive (aneesaluva@gmail.com) under a folder named TravelFinanceApp/. The folder contains:
travel_data.json — App A data (members, trips, documents)
TravelFinanceApp_Mirror/ — auto-backup folder with tiered snapshots

File IDs are stored in the owner's browser localStorage. On a new device, the app searches Drive by folder name to find the files. If the folder is renamed or moved, the app will create new empty files.
localSave() — always use this for user data writes
CSV/Excel parser + column mapper for both apps
Transaction list — contains retry logic for null data
Version stamp script — run before every package
find . -name "*.js" | grep -v .git | while read f; do node --check "$f"; done
Run the Python import checker script (see Section 9.1)
End of Handover Document — v3.5.5 | 22 March 2026
